title: Fix flavour mapping (AQI showing as Premium) and unit count off-by-one in reports

requirements.summary: Fix the flavour value not being correctly saved or read during CSV/XLS import: ensure that when 'AQI' is selected in CSVImportSection, it persists as 'aqi' in ReportEntry records and displays correctly in the Reports table
acceptanceCriteria: Selecting 'AQI' flavour in CSVImportSection and uploading files results in all imported records storing 'AQI' as the flavour value in the backend.
The Reports table displays 'AQI' in the Flavour column for those records — not 'Premium' or any other value.
Other flavour selections (e.g., 'Premium', 'Others') are still saved and displayed correctly.
Re-uploading with a different flavour selection updates the flavour for affected records correctly.

file_operations.description: Fix the flavour mapping logic in the handleSubmit function to correctly map the selected flavour value ('AQI', 'Premium', 'Deluxe', 'Standard', or custom 'Others' value) to the backend Flavour enum format ('aqi', 'premium', 'deluxe', 'standard') before calling directUpsert. Ensure the flavour Select component value state and the mapping to backend enum values are consistent. Verify that the customFlavour input correctly handles 'Others' selection.

Review and ensure the parseCSVToReportEntries function does not override or ignore the flavour parameter passed to it. The function should use the flavour argument directly when constructing parsed records, not infer or default it from CSV content. Verify that the flavour value flows correctly through to the returned array of tuples.

Ensure the Flavour column renders the flavour enum value correctly by mapping backend enum values ('aqi', 'premium', 'deluxe', 'standard') to display-friendly labels ('AQI', 'Premium', 'Deluxe', 'Standard') with proper capitalization. Add a helper function or inline mapping to transform the flavour value for display purposes only.

Review the directUpsert mutation to confirm it correctly passes flavour values to the backend upsertBatchReport function without transformation or default overrides. Ensure the tuple construction in the mutation uses the flavour parameter as-is and does not apply any incorrect mapping or fallback to 'premium'.

requirements.summary: Fix the unit count mismatch in Reports: ensure that when 4 distinct unit files are uploaded, all 4 records are stored and displayed, and the Reports table footer correctly shows 'Totals (4 units)'
acceptanceCriteria: Uploading 4 CSV/XLS files each containing a distinct unit ID results in exactly 4 records stored in the backend.
The Reports table for that week displays all 4 unit rows.
The totals row in the Reports table footer reads 'Totals (4 units)'.
No unit record is silently dropped or merged with another during batch upsert.
The distinct unit count logic in reportFilters.ts (or equivalent) does not under-count when all unit IDs are valid and distinct.

file_operations.description: Review and fix the countDistinctUnits function to ensure it correctly counts all valid, distinct unit IDs without off-by-one errors. Verify that the INVALID_UNIT_IDS set is accurate and does not inadvertently include valid unit IDs. Check the filtering logic in filterValidUnits to ensure no valid units are excluded. Add defensive checks to prevent under-counting.

Verify that the totals row calculation correctly uses the distinct unit count from reportFilters.ts and displays it as 'Totals (N units)' in the footer. Ensure the table renders all rows from the filteredEntries prop without accidentally skipping or merging rows with the same unit ID but different week/year combinations. Review the sorting and rendering logic to confirm all distinct records are displayed.

Review the parseCSVToReportEntries function to ensure it does not silently drop or merge records during parsing. Verify that each valid unit ID from each uploaded file produces a separate tuple in the output array, even if multiple files contain the same unit ID for different weeks. Check for any deduplication logic that might incorrectly merge records during parsing.

Review the handleSubmit function to ensure that when multiple files are uploaded, each file's parsed records are correctly accumulated and passed to directUpsert without dropping any entries. Verify that the batch upsert call receives all parsed tuples and does not silently fail on any subset. Add validation or logging to confirm the number of records being submitted matches the number of files uploaded.
